title: Abolboda granularis
intro: Abolboda granularis, also known as Uvularia granularis, is a species of flowering plant in the family Colchicaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, inflammation, and respiratory issues, due to its reported analgesic, antiseptic, and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Abolboda granularis include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic compounds, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal properties. Herbal preparations made from Abolboda granularis include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are used to treat coughs, colds, and other respiratory problems.
taxonomy: Abolboda granularis is a species of flowering plant in the family Bromeliaceae. It belongs to the genus Abolboda. Abolboda granularis is native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The classification of Abolboda granularis is further specified as a member of the order Poales and class Liliopsida. Abolboda granularis is also known by its synonyms, including Abolboda bracteata and Abolboda minor.
